> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-810479 Sprint 30 вАФ Pemberton RFQ pack + redline c08/03-pricing-schedule.docx`

# Pricing Schedule

Pemberton Engineering Ltd.¬†вАФ Indirect MRO Framework вАФ 3-Year Term

**Document 03 of 7 вАФ to be completed by the Bidder and returned with bid**

**Pemberton ref:** PEL/PROC/2026-RFQ-073 **Issued:** 11 May 2026

This Pricing Schedule forms part of the Master Supply Agreement (Document 02). All prices to be quoted **ex-VAT in GBP** for UK destinations and in **EUR** for EU destinations. Bidders must complete every line; вАЬPOAвАЭ or вАЬTBCвАЭ responses will result in disqualification.

# 1. Category Scope

The framework covers the following indirect MRO categories:

Cat | Category | Indicative Annual Spend (¬£)
A | Cutting tools and abrasives | 4,200,000
B | Industrial fasteners | 2,800,000
C | Lubricants and process fluids | 3,400,000
D | PPE and workwear | 1,900,000
E | Electrical components and motors | 3,100,000
F | Pneumatics and hydraulics | 2,000,000
G | Capital spares (nominated SKU list) | 1,000,000
 | **Total indicative annual spend** | **18,400,000**

Spend figures are based on the twelve months ending 31 March 2026 and are indicative only. No commitment is made to any minimum volume.

# 2. Ceiling Prices

For each SKU in Appendix A (the SKU master list вАФ 1,247 SKUs), bidders must quote a unit ceiling price. The ceiling price represents the maximum the Bidder may charge during the first twelve (12) months of the Term, irrespective of actual market price movements.

# 3. Rebate Ladder

Bidders shall propose a rebate structure on the following ladder. Rebates accrue on aggregated group spend (across all UK and EU sites) per calendar quarter and are paid in arrears within thirty (30) days of quarter end by credit note.

Tier | Quarterly aggregate spend (¬£) | Min. rebate %
1 | Up to 750,000 | 0.5%
2 | 750,001 вАУ 1,500,000 | 1.5%
3 | 1,500,001 вАУ 2,500,000 | 2.5%
4 | Above 2,500,000 | 4.0%

Bidders may propose higher rebate percentages or additional tiers in their bid response.

# 4. Volume Discount Triggers

Quarterly volume discounts above the rebate ladder apply automatically on the following category-level triggers:

* Category A spend exceeds ¬£1.2m in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category A

* Category C spend exceeds ¬£1.0m in a quarter: additional 0.75% on Category C

* Category E spend exceeds ¬£900k in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category E

# 5. Currency and Payment

5.1 UK invoices in GBP. EU invoices in EUR at the local siteвАЩs billing entity.

5.2 Foreign exchange risk is borne by the Supplier. No FX-related price adjustments are permitted within the first twelve (12) months.

5.3 Payment terms per MSA clause 6 (90 days from receipt of valid invoice).

# 6. Open-Book Costing for Top-50 SKUs

For the fifty (50) highest-spend SKUs as identified by Buyer from time to time, Supplier shall provide open-book costing comprising landed cost, Supplier margin, and any rebates received from upstream manufacturers. Buyer reserves the right to require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds twelve percent (12%) on any open-book SKU.

# 7. Most-Favoured-Customer

Supplier represents that, for substantially comparable Goods supplied on substantially comparable terms, the prices quoted under this Pricing Schedule are no less favourable than those offered to any other customer of Supplier. Supplier shall promptly notify Buyer of any improvement in pricing offered to a third party and shall extend such improvement to Buyer.

# 8. Pricing Schedule Return Format

Bidders shall return Appendix A as an Excel workbook with the columns set out in the template. PDF returns will not be accepted. Bidders must also provide:

* A written justification for any SKU where the quoted ceiling price exceeds BuyerвАЩs internal benchmark by more than twenty percent (20%) (Buyer will share benchmark deltas during the clarification window with shortlisted bidders);

* An explanation of methodology for any FX, freight, or duty assumptions baked into landed cost;

* A statement of any sustainability premia included in pricing (e.g.¬†low-carbon steel, recycled-content fasteners).

# Appendix A вАФ SKU Master List (Extract)

SKU | Description | UoM | Indicative annual qty | Bid ceiling price (¬£)
A-0001 | HSS twist drill bit, 6mm | each | 12,400 | __________
A-0002 | Carbide insert, CCMT 09T308 | each | 38,200 | __________

B-0044 | M12 hex bolt, A2-70, 60mm | each | 184,000 | __________
C-0017 | ISO VG 68 hydraulic oil | 208L drum | 412 | __________
D-0202 | Cut-resistant glove level D, size L | pair | 24,800 | __________
E-0091 | 3-phase induction motor 5.5kW IE3 | each | 92 | __________
F-0028 | Pneumatic cylinder ISO 15552 80x200 | each | 148 | __________
G-0007 | Bearing 6308-2RS | each | 2,420 | __________

_[Full SKU master list of 1,247 lines provided as separate Excel workbook on portal вАФ extract above is illustrative.]_

# Pricing Schedule

Pemberton Engineering Ltd.¬†вАФ Indirect MRO Framework вАФ 3-Year Term

**Document 03 of 7 вАФ to be completed by the Bidder and returned with bid**

**Pemberton ref:** PEL/PROC/2026-RFQ-073 **Issued:** 11 May 2026

This Pricing Schedule forms part of the Master Supply Agreement (Document 02). All prices to be quoted **ex-VAT in GBP** for UK destinations and in **EUR** for EU destinations. Bidders must complete every line; вАЬPOAвАЭ or вАЬTBCвАЭ responses will result in disqualification.

# 1. Category Scope

The framework covers the following indirect MRO categories:

Cat | Category | Indicative Annual Spend (¬£)
A | Cutting tools and abrasives | 4,200,000
B | Industrial fasteners | 2,800,000
C | Lubricants and process fluids | 3,400,000
D | PPE and workwear | 1,900,000
E | Electrical components and motors | 3,100,000
F | Pneumatics and hydraulics | 2,000,000
G | Capital spares (nominated SKU list) | 1,000,000
 | **Total indicative annual spend** | **18,400,000**

Spend figures are based on the twelve months ending 31 March 2026 and are indicative only. No commitment is made to any minimum volume.

# 2. Ceiling Prices

For each SKU in Appendix A (the SKU master list вАФ 1,247 SKUs), bidders must quote a unit ceiling price. The ceiling price represents the maximum the Bidder may charge during the first twelve (12) months of the Term, irrespective of actual market price movements.

# 3. Rebate Ladder

Bidders shall propose a rebate structure on the following ladder. Rebates accrue on aggregated group spend (across all UK and EU sites) per calendar quarter and are paid in arrears within thirty (30) days of quarter end by credit note.

Tier | Quarterly aggregate spend (¬£) | Min. rebate %
1 | Up to 750,000 | 0.5%
2 | 750,001 вАУ 1,500,000 | 1.5%
3 | 1,500,001 вАУ 2,500,000 | 2.5%
4 | Above 2,500,000 | 4.0%

Bidders may propose higher rebate percentages or additional tiers in their bid response.

# 4. Volume Discount Triggers

Quarterly volume discounts above the rebate ladder apply automatically on the following category-level triggers:

* Category A spend exceeds ¬£1.2m in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category A

* Category C spend exceeds ¬£1.0m in a quarter: additional 0.75% on Category C

* Category E spend exceeds ¬£900k in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category E

# 5. Currency and Payment

5.1 UK invoices in GBP. EU invoices in EUR at the local siteвАЩs billing entity.

5.2 Foreign exchange risk is borne by the Supplier. No FX-related price adjustments are permitted within the first twelve (12) months.

5.3 Payment terms per MSA clause 6 (90 days from receipt of valid invoice).

# 6. Open-Book Costing for Top-50 SKUs

For the fifty (50) highest-spend SKUs as identified by Buyer from time to time, Supplier shall provide open-book costing comprising landed cost, Supplier margin, and any rebates received from upstream manufacturers. Buyer reserves the right to require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds twelve percent (12%) on any open-book SKU.

# 7. Most-Favoured-Customer

Supplier represents that, for substantially comparable Goods supplied on substantially comparable terms, the prices quoted under this Pricing Schedule are no less favourable than those offered to any other customer of Supplier. Supplier shall promptly notify Buyer of any improvement in pricing offered to a third party and shall extend such improvement to Buyer.

# 8. Pricing Schedule Return Format

Bidders shall return Appendix A as an Excel workbook with the columns set out in the template. PDF returns will not be accepted. Bidders must also provide:

* A written justification for any SKU where the quoted ceiling price exceeds BuyerвАЩs internal benchmark by more than twenty percent (20%) (Buyer will share benchmark deltas during the clarification window with shortlisted bidders);

* An explanation of methodology for any FX, freight, or duty assumptions baked into landed cost;

* A statement of any sustainability premia included in pricing (e.g.¬†low-carbon steel, recycled-content fasteners).

# Appendix A вАФ SKU Master List (Extract)

SKU | Description | UoM | Indicative annual qty | Bid ceiling price (¬£)
A-0001 | HSS twist drill bit, 6mm | each | 12,400 | __________
A-0002 | Carbide insert, CCMT 09T308 | each | 38,200 | __________
A-0103 | Diamond abrasive disc, 230mm | each | 6,900 | __________
B-0044 | M12 hex bolt, A2-70, 60mm | each | 184,000 | __________
C-0017 | ISO VG 68 hydraulic oil | 208L drum | 412 | __________
D-0202 | Cut-resistant glove level D, size L | pair | 24,800 | __________
E-0091 | 3-phase induction motor 5.5kW IE3 | each | 92 | __________
F-0028 | Pneumatic cylinder ISO 15552 80x200 | each | 148 | __________
G-0007 | Bearing 6308-2RS | each | 2,420 | __________

_[Full SKU master list of 1,247 lines provided as separate Excel workbook on portal вАФ extract above is illustrative.]_

---

<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

blocks.text:
Excellent. I now have the full picture. Let me synthesize the playbook analysis and deliver the lawyer's read:

---

## **PLAYBOOK ANALYSIS COMPLETE**

**Read:** This RFQ pack is a **heavily buyer-favoured, procurement-led framework** with structural asymmetries on nearly every material front. The GTCs are the dominant instrument (they override the MSA). Payment terms, IP assignment, audit scope, SLAs with service credits, and compliance obligations are all aggressive by market standard.

Let me give you the three tiers you need for decision-making:

---

### **рЯФі WALK-AWAY ISSUES (3)**

These are non-negotiable if you want the deal. If Pemberton won't move, the bid economics don't survive.

**1. Uncapped Indemnity (Clause 10) + Excluded Liability Carve-Out (Clause 11.3)**

The indemnity runs to breach of contract, negligence, defects, IP infringement, *and* breach of any applicable law вАФ uncapped and surviving termination. But when you flip to the liability cap at 11.1, Supplier is capped at 12 months of fees **except** the indemnity stays uncapped. Then 11.3 says economic loss is excluded *unless* it arises from the indemnity, confidentiality, compliance, data protection, or audit. 

**This reads as: your indemnity is uncapped but your general liability is capped.** The tail wags the dog. Over an ¬£18m annual spend, a material IP claim (even if frivolous) wipes the cap and exposes you to tens of millions. This is uninsurable.

**Push:** Either (i) cap the IP indemnity at 12 months of fees (carve-out from the 12-month cap), or (ii) negotiate a separate, lower cap on indemnity (e.g., ¬£2m aggregate), or (iii) walk. You cannot bid on uncapped IP risk without massive risk insurance premium.

---

**2. Foreground IP Assignment вАФ Absolute & Perpetual (Clause 9.2)**

All Foreground IP вАФ including improvements, modifications, derivatives arising from the performance вАФ **vest absolutely in Buyer on creation**. You assign with full title guarantee and future rights. And look at 9.4: Buyer gets a perpetual royalty-free sublicensable licence to your Background IP "to the extent embedded."

Combined with the MSA's order-of-precedence clause (20.1), which says GTCs prevail, the GTCs' IP clause (which I saw in the GTCs: "Any Foreground IP arising in the performance of any Order vests in Buyer absolutely on creation") is the controlling language.

**The issue:** If you have any proprietary methodologies, data analytics, supply-chain optimizations, or customized software embedded in how you deliver (e.g., a cost-reduction algorithm you've developed), you're assigning it to Pemberton forever and granting it to all their competitors via the sublicense right. This is industry-standard for pure product supply but **hostile for services or custom solutions**.

**Ask first:** Will Pemberton accept a carve-out for your pre-existing tools, templates, processes, and know-how? If Pemberton says no, you need commercial sign-off that the IP exposure is worth the margin.

---

**3. Termination for Convenience with Only 5% Wind-Down (Clause 18.1)**

Buyer can terminate without cause on 30 days' notice, and your recovery is capped at reimbursement of direct costs up to 5% of work-in-progress on termination date. On an ¬£18m annual spend, this caps your wind-down at ~¬£75k (5% of ~¬£1.5m monthly WIP). That's not enough to cover demobilization, stranded inventory, customer backlog on non-movable goods, or retained staff.

**And:** The GTCs (clause 5 in the GTCs I saw: "Buyer may terminate any Order...") let Pemberton cancel individual orders at any time with only 15% reimbursement of direct costs. So they can death-by-a-thousand-cuts you, order by order.

**Push:** Either (i) require Pemberton to give you 90 days' termination notice (not 30) to allow redeployment, (ii) increase the wind-down cap to 10-15% of monthly spend (or 3 months of margin), or (iii) get a minimum purchase commitment (e.g., 70% of forecast spend per year, averaged over the first two years). Without one of these, you're pricing at risk-adjusted returns, and the bid won't be competitive.

---

### **рЯЯ† HIGH-PRIORITY PUSHBACK ITEMS (5)**

These are material and should be pushed hard. They are moveable if you have leverage (e.g., you're one of two qualified bidders, or your pricing is significantly better).

**1. 90-Day Payment Terms (Clause 6.2)**

Market standard for MRO supply is 30вАУ45 days net. Pemberton wants 90. Over ¬£18m annual spend, that's ¬£1.35m in unpaid receivables at any given time. Combined with rebates paid in arrears within 30 days of quarter end (Pricing Schedule ¬І3), you're effectively financing Pemberton's inventory by 4+ months.

**Push:** Propose 60 days net as compromise, or tie payment terms to invoice size (e.g., 45 days for invoices under ¬£50k, 60 days for larger). If Pemberton insists on 90, price a 2% early-payment discount to incent faster pay, or load working-capital cost into your bid.

---

**2. Price Benchmarking & Most-Favoured-Customer (Clause 5.4 MSA + Clause 7 Pricing Schedule)**

Buyer reserves the right "at any time" to benchmark prices and require you to match any verified third-party offer. And the Pricing Schedule (¬І7) locks you into an MFC clause: prices are "no less favourable" than any other customer on comparable terms. Buyer can audit you.

**Problem:** This creates continuous exposure. If a competitor undercuts you in a separate bid cycle, Buyer can demand the new price retroactively (or at next renewal). Coupled with the open-book costing (Pricing Schedule ¬І6), which lets Buyer compress your margin above 12%, you're under perpetual margin pressure.

**Push:** (i) Narrow MFC to customers in the same geography and sector (e.g., "manufacturing in the UK"). (ii) Exclude promotional or time-limited pricing. (iii) Require written notice and 30 days to respond before price adjustment takes effect. (iv) Carve out the top-50 open-book SKUs from MFC (i.e., agree they're subject to margin review separately). (v) Propose annual (not "at any time") benchmarking windows.

---

**3. Audit Scope & Cost (Clause 16 MSA + Compliance Annex)**

Buyer can audit "on giving five (5) Business Days' notice" your records, premises, systems, *and personnel*. Auditors can be Buyer's employees, affiliates, or third-party auditors. Supplier bears all costs. Audit rights survive termination for 6 years.

The Compliance Annex goes further: Buyer can audit supply-chain compliance (modern slavery, sanctions, bribery) at Supplier's sub-suppliers and business partners with 24-hour notice (implied in "cooperate fully"). And Buyer's auditors can demand access to personnel records, payroll, workplace inspections, and certifications.

**Problem:** 5 Business Days is not workable for a complex operations audit (premises, systems, supply chain). And you're paying for Buyer's auditors to review not just your performance but your sub-suppliers' labour practicesвАФa scope creep risk. Plus the 6-year survival means Buyer can audit you for claims 6 years after you've been off the contract.

**Push:** (i) Increase notice to 20 Business Days (or 10 for emergency/compliance audits). (ii) Buyer bears audit costs for scheduled audits; Supplier bears costs only for audits triggered by a suspected breach. (iii) Limit survival to 3 years post-termination. (iv) Exclude sub-supplier labour audits unless Buyer has reasonable suspicion of a breach (don't do Buyer's supply-chain due diligence). (v) Require Buyer's auditor to be bound by confidentiality and to limit findings to Buyer's employees (no sharing with competitors via Pemberton's group).

---

**4. SLA Service Credits & Escalation (Clause 3, SLA)**

The SLA is tied to OTIF (on-time, in-full) delivery targets. If you miss OTIF three times in a rolling 12-month period, that triggers material breach (clause 18.2 of the MSAвАФtermination for cause). And service credits are aggressive: 2% rebate for missing targets in any month, compounding to 20% rebate (capped) if you're consistently out. Emergency break response gets 4 hours on-site, with escalation to Pemberton's CFO and your CEO if there's a second SLA breach in 6 months.

**Problem:** (i) Three strikes and you're out for termination feels hair-triggerвАФindustrial suppliers typically get 2вАУ3 material breaches per year as normal variance. (ii) The escalation matrix (Site Maintenance Lead вЖТ Account Manager вЖТ Pemberton Purchasing Manager вЖТ CFO / CEO level) is theatrical and designed to create internal pressure to concede. (iii) The service credit arithmetic: 2% √Ч 4 months + emergency response penalty can add up to an effective 15вАУ20% margin hit in a bad quarter, which kills profitability.

**Push:** (i) Material breach should require *two consecutive months* of OTIF miss (not three), OR two breaches in a rolling 18-month period. (ii) Cap service credits to 5% of monthly invoice value (not compounding). (iii) Exclude force majeure and supplier-caused delays (e.g., if Pemberton changes order specs last-minute, that's not an OTIF miss). (iv) Remove the CEO/CFO escalation for routine misses; escalate only for material breaches confirmed in writing.

---

**5. Compliance & ESG Attestation at Director Level (Compliance Annex)**

You must provide an ESG attestation signed at director level every April, confirming modern slavery compliance, Bribery Act compliance, sanctions screening, ISO 14001 certification, and by Year 3, a published Net Zero target validated under SBTi. Any material non-compliance becomes grounds for material breach (Annex ¬ІFailure to Comply).

**Problem:** (i) Director-level attestation is high-stakesвАФit carries personal liability if wrong. (ii) SBTi validation is expensive and takes 6+ months; committing to it in Year 3 is ambitious if you don't already have one. (iii) The form template provided in Appendix C of the Annex requires disclosure of Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions with a published commitmentвАФthat's a lot of data Pemberton can use in negotiations or share with competitors. (iv) "Material non-compliance" is undefinedвАФdoes a single workplace incident = material breach?

**Push:** (i) Soften language to "вА¶to the best of [Supplier]'s knowledge and belief based on reasonable inquiry." (ii) For ESG targets (Net Zero, SBTi), propose a conditional commitment: "вА¶Supplier shall use reasonable endeavours to pursue SBTi validation by [DATE], with continued compliance review after." (iii) Define "material non-compliance" as "non-compliance that results in a regulatory fine, legal judgment, or public enforcement action." (iv) Exclude disclosure of detailed Scope 3 emissions data unless contractually required by a third-party audit; propose a summary-level attestation only.

---

### **вЬЕ ACCEPTABLE / NICE-TO-HAVE ITEMS**

**Standard or navigable:**

* **Non-exclusive appointment (Clause 2.1)** вАФ standard for indirect MRO; Pemberton will have competing suppliers. Not a pushback point.

* **No minimum volume commitment (Clause 2.2)** вАФ harsh but typical in procurement RFQs. Offset this with strong OTIF performance to build switching costs.

* **Pricing firm for first 12 months, then annual CPI (Clause 5.2)** вАФ standard. Proposal to cap increases at UK CPI is reasonable; 2.5% floor is tight but market. Acceptable as-is if you hedge FX upside in Year 2вАУ3 pricing.

* **DDP Incoterms to receiving site (Clause 7.1)** вАФ you're paying freight. Price accordingly. Standard for UK industrial supply.

* **24-month warranty on Goods (Clause 8.3)** вАФ standard for industrial components. Acceptable.

* **English law, Manchester courts (Clause 19)** вАФ domestic litigation forum. Fine for a UK bidder.

* **GTCs prevail over MSA (Clause 20.1)** вАФ this is loaded (see battle-of-forms risk below), but it's disclosed and you can read the GTCs. You're choosing to accept it when you bid.

---

### **вЪ†пЄП BATTLE-OF-FORMS RISK (Governance)**

**Critical point:** The order of precedence (Clause 20.1 MSA + GTCs Clause covering "Entire Agreement") ranks documents as:

1. **GTCs (prevail over all)**
2. MSA
3. SLA
4. Compliance Annex
5. Pricing Schedule
6. Any individual Order

This means the GTCsвАФwhich you sawвАФcontain terms that override the MSA. For example, the GTCs say "Buyer may terminate any Order at any time for any reason on 15 days' written notice" (GTCs Clause 5, Termination). But the MSA says 30 days. **The GTCs win.** Similarly, the GTCs say "Buyer's aggregate liability shall not exceed ¬£100,000" (GTCs Clause 11), but the MSA says the Supplier's cap is 12 months' fees. Since the GTCs prevail, the Buyer's liability exposure is actually capped at ¬£100k, while your liability is uncapped for indemnities and ¬£18m cap for everything else. **That's highly asymmetric.**

**Push:** Propose a revised order of precedence:

> (i) MSA (as mutually agreed in this bid); (ii) Pricing Schedule; (iii) SLA; (iv) GTCs, *provided that any GTCs clause conflicting with Sections 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, 16, 18 of the MSA shall be read subject to the MSA*; (v) any Order.

In other words: keep the GTCs as backstop, but carve out the high-risk sections (IP, indemnity, liability, compliance, data, audit, termination) to MSA control.

---

### **рЯУЛ NEXT STEPS & ESCALATION**

**To your commercial sponsor (Head of Procurement / VP Supply Chain):**

1. **Confirm bid strategy:** Are you bidding to win, or bidding to position for a second wave? If this is a strategic win, accept that you'll absorb some of these terms. If you're testing the market, push harder on items 1, 2, 3 above.

2. **Get clarification on volume:** The ¬£18.4m is "indicative." Confirm with Pemberton (during the clarification window, which is **by 17 June per the invitation letter**) whether they'll commit to a minimum annual purchase (e.g., 70% of forecast) or if it's truly free-form. Bid differently based on the answer.

3. **Pricing strategy:** The open-book costing (top 50 SKUs) + MFC clause + benchmarking rights mean your margins will be continuously compressed. Price higher on the tail items (G category, specialty fasteners, emergency stock) where they have less visibility and more inelastic demand.

4. **Insurance & risk:** Get a quote for E&O / product liability insurance covering uncapped indemnity exposure. If the premium is >2% of bid margin, you're underpricing. Consider requiring Pemberton to obtain product liability insurance for Goods you supply (or split the premium).

**To GC (escalation):**

* **IP assignment:** Confirm with product/engineering whether you're comfortable assigning all Foreground IP (process improvements, supply-chain optimization) to Pemberton forever. If not, this is a deal-killer and needs early disclosure.

* **Termination/wind-down:** The 5% wind-down cap is low. If Pemberton is strategic to your supply-chain strategy (i.e., they justify significant fixed investment in tooling, staff, or inventory), escalate to CFO to confirm the bid economics still work with a 30-day-out scenario.

* **Compliance/ESG:** The director-level ESG attestation creates personal liability. Confirm your CFO and COO are comfortable signing. If not, this needs pushback at the top level.

---

### **рЯУК SCORING SUMMARY**

| Dimension | Rating | Comment |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **Commercial terms** | рЯФі Aggressive | 90-day payment, no min volume, termination-at-will, uncapped indemnity = cash-flow negative + risk exposure. Bid at 8вАУ12% margin premium to offset. |
| **Service requirements** | рЯЯ° Medium | OTIF targets reasonable but escalation matrix is theatrical. SLA credits are compounding but not industry-outlier if you're operationally mature. |
| **IP/Data** | рЯФі Hostile | Absolute assignment of Foreground IP + Background IP licence + open-book costing + audit rights = you're surrendering competitive advantage. Flag to exec sponsor. |
| **Compliance/ESG** | рЯЯ° Medium | Director-level attestation is high-stakes; SBTi commitment by Year 3 is aggressive but doable if you plan ahead. Scope 3 disclosure is data-rich; push for summary attestation. |
| **Governance** | рЯФі Imbalanced | GTCs override MSA, creating asymmetric liability (Supplier uncapped, Buyer capped at ¬£100k). Negotiate order of precedence. |
| **Overall bid confidence** | рЯЯ° Conditional | Winnable if (1) you push walk-aways + priority items pre-bid, (2) you price at risk-adjusted margin, (3) you have exec alignment on IP/ESG exposure. Otherwise: bid defensively or decline. |

---

**My read:** This is a **market-leading buyer** with deep procurement sophistication and a willingness to push suppliers hard on cost, risk, and governance. They've drafted the GTCs and MSA to be buyer-protective. You'll win this deal on **price + operational excellence + willingness to absorb compliance burden**, not on contract terms. 

Negotiate hard on the three walk-aways and the five priority items. If Pemberton moves 40вАУ60% toward your position, it's a viable deal. If they won't budge, your margin needs to reflect the uncompensated riskвАФand that might not be competitive.

**Suggested approach:** Submit written clarification questions on items 1, 2, 3 during the June 17 deadline, stating your position without conceding. Use Pemberton's response to calibrate your final bid risk premium. Then decide.
What's our exposure if their GTCs (Document 05) override the MSA on indemnities? Worst case, what are we signing up to?
Let me read the GTCs carefully to map the exact indemnity exposure.
